name: Browserbase Cloud Browser MCP Server (ImRonAI)
what_it_does: MCP server for Browserbase cloud browser automation — enables AI agents to automate cloud-hosted browsers to navigate websites, interact with elements, and extract data. Provides scalable headless browser infrastructure for AI-driven web automation without managing local browser infrastructure.
use_cases: Automating web scraping and data extraction from websites using cloud browsers
Testing web applications with AI-driven browser interaction from QA agents
Navigating and extracting information from JavaScript-heavy web applications
Building web automation workflows at scale using Browserbase's cloud infrastructure
not_for: Sites prohibiting automated access (verify robots.txt and ToS)
High-frequency trading or financial automation via browser (use APIs instead)
Local browser testing requiring specific browser configurations
best_when: An AI agent needs to interact with websites that don't have APIs — Browserbase provides managed cloud browser infrastructure with Playwright/Puppeteer support for scalable web automation.
avoid_when: Target websites have APIs available — direct API calls are faster and cheaper. Also: automated web access may violate ToS for some sites.

auth.notes: Browserbase API key and project ID required. Available from Browserbase dashboard. Community MCP implementation wrapping official Browserbase SDK.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Browserbase free tier: limited browser minutes
pricing.paid_tiers: Browserbase Pro and Team tiers for higher usage
pricing.notes: Browserbase cloud browser infrastructure charges per session minute. Community MCP server is free open source.

known_agent_gotchas: Web automation ToS compliance: many websites prohibit automated access — verify before deploying
Browser session costs accumulate with usage — monitor session minutes and costs
CAPTCHA and bot detection: many sites will block automated browser access
Session state management: browser sessions are not persistent by default — handle state carefully
Community MCP implementation — validate against current Browserbase SDK version
agent_readiness.security_notes: Web page content (including form data and cookies) accessible to cloud browser. Never use for pages with high-security credentials. ToS compliance critical.

security_score_components.security_notes: Cloud browser accesses web content including sensitive pages. Avoid high-security workflows. ToS compliance required. SOC2 certified Browserbase infrastructure.
